compiled_state.author: Joe Oakhart
compiled_state.description:
## Defect

`rebar review-plan` crashes on the attestation-persistence path with:

```
TypeError: _with_index_lock_retry() got an unexpected keyword argument 'force_reclaim'
```

(observed exit code 11) when two or more `review-plan` runs race the ticket-index lock
concurrently — e.g. several plan reviews launched in parallel. The signing/persist step calls
`_with_index_lock_retry(..., force_reclaim=...)` but the retry helper does not accept that
keyword, so the attestation write aborts.

## Impact

- The review body completes and `signature.signed` may be reported `true` on stdout, but the
  attestation is **not persisted** to the store — `rebar audit show <id>` shows no signature.
  So stdout `signed=true` is not a reliable indicator of a durable signature under contention.
- Forces plan-review gating to be run strictly serially to avoid the crash; parallel review of
  an epic + its children (the natural batch operation) is unsafe.

## Repro (observed)

1. Launch >=2 `rebar review-plan <id>` runs concurrently against tickets sharing the index lock.
2. One or more crash with the `force_reclaim` TypeError (EXIT=11) on the persist step.

## Suggested fix direction

Reconcile the caller/callee signatures on the index-lock retry path
(`_with_index_lock_retry` and the attestation-persist caller in the workflow/orchestrator
layer): either add/accept the `force_reclaim` parameter on `_with_index_lock_retry` or stop
passing it. Add a regression test that persists an attestation while the index lock is
contended.

## Acceptance criteria

- [ ] Concurrent `review-plan` runs contending the index lock persist their attestations
  without raising `TypeError: ... force_reclaim`.
- [ ] `rebar audit show <id>` reflects the persisted signature after a contended review.
- [ ] A regression test exercises the contended-persist path.
compiled_state.title: review-plan attestation-persist crashes: _with_index_lock_retry() unexpected kwarg force_reclaim under lock contention
